name: The White House
subject: 'America First is about unity’
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=cdd40422c6992c06693ae3479646709004e3172f1802eeb44c6e94f32029c7d9d694424309e2901c7c0a045fbbb05ca65cdcb45d5a1d6b54

'America First is about unity’

Speaking at the JW Marriott hotel in Washington, D.C., yesterday, President Donald J. Trump told members of the Latino business community that a shared National vision brings together people of all backgrounds.

“America First is about unity,” the President said. “It’s about coming together as one family—one big, beautiful American family—no matter our race, or color, or creed, to protect our jobs, our communities, and our country.”

The Latino community has been doing particularly well under that vision:

- Last year, the unemployment rate for Hispanics reached its lowest level in history.
- Latinos are also starting new businesses at three times the national average.
- More than 8 in 10 Latino voters share President Trump’s belief that immigration to the United States should be based on skill, not just personal relationships.

President Trump, who knew many of the attendees personally, ended his remarks on a note of appreciation and optimism. “This is our time. This is our moment.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=cdd40422c6992c0666997a01e0567104a6514ffa155ab7b9c00254eda4c7f19f4efb585586d2a3ffa143d5a17c1c4173889f6afb8b4ed0d7
Watch the President’s full speech to the Latino business community.

Stopping crime before it starts

During his State of the Union Address in January, President Trump told the Nation that prison reform would be a priority for his Administration in 2018. “This year we will embark on reforming our prisons to help former inmates who have served their time get a second chance,” the President said.

Pat Nolan, director of the American Conservative Union Foundation’s Center for Criminal Justice Reform, said President Trump’s declaration was "a milestone in the growing conservative movement to apply conservative principles to our justice system," Reason magazine reports.

Yesterday, the President took another step toward that goal, issuing an Executive Order to establish a Federal Interagency Council on Crime Prevention and Improving Reentry. “While investigating crimes and prosecuting perpetrators must remain the top priority of law enforcement,” the Order states, “crime reduction policy should also include efforts to prevent crime in the first place and to lower recidivism rates.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=cdd40422c6992c066fadadb3c552db25a4ae0d19c8d08e86cb55ce2c992081fd69bdfe4f908881e6b2aa0aa90d19cfb1589d0c1405dba319
Read more about the Executive Order and what the Interagency Council will do.

At the center of America’s resurgence

It’s impossible to talk about the record-breaking successes of the U.S. economy today without talking about tax cuts. Unemployment is at its lowest rate in 17 years. Jobless claims recently hit a 48-year low. In January alone, the U.S. economy added 200,000 jobs.

“At the center of America’s resurgence are the massive tax cuts that we just passed before Christmas,” President Trump says. The evidence backs him up. Third-party consumer and small-business confidence indices have hit their highest marks in years this quarter. More than 4 million American workers are receiving bonuses, wage increases, or other new benefits as a direct result of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act.

And the best part? As the positive impacts of tax reform multiply, more and more businesses have begun to turn their attention to the long-term investments that matter most for a healthy economy.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=cdd40422c6992c06f20c3a4aa84664ea0ca384df2b1dedc5b6cf388ab29642cdb1f7187a350db4b25e9626ac0f4b6ca37ca36f4eae1bbb1d
Learn more about how tax reform is delivering for all Americans.

Photo of the Day

Vice President Mike Pence | March 7, 2018 (Official White House Photo by D. Myles Cullen)

POTUS TODAY

This morning, President Trump will hold a meeting with his Cabinet. Later, the President, along with Vice President Pence, will host a roundtable with members of Congress and video game industry leaders.

clean_body: 1600 Daily 'America First is about unity’ Speaking at the JW Marriott hotel in Washington, D.C., yesterday, President Donald J. Trump told members of the Latino business community that a shared National vision brings together people of all backgrounds. “America First is about unity,” the President said. “It’s about coming together as one family—one big, beautiful American family—no matter our race, or color, or creed, to protect our jobs, our communities, and our country.” The Latino community has been doing particularly well under that vision: - Last year, the unemployment rate for Hispanics reached its lowest level in history. - Latinos are also starting new businesses at three times the national average. - More than 8 in 10 Latino voters share President Trump’s belief that immigration to the United States should be based on skill, not just personal relationships. President Trump, who knew many of the attendees personally, ended his remarks on a note of appreciation and optimism. “This is our time. This is our moment.” Watch the President’s full speech to the Latino business community. Stopping crime before it starts During his State of the Union Address in January, President Trump told the Nation that prison reform would be a priority for his Administration in 2018. “This year we will embark on reforming our prisons to help former inmates who have served their time get a second chance,” the President said. Pat Nolan, director of the American Conservative Union Foundation’s Center for Criminal Justice Reform, said President Trump’s declaration was "a milestone in the growing conservative movement to apply conservative principles to our justice system," Reason magazine reports. Yesterday, the President took another step toward that goal, issuing an Executive Order to establish a Federal Interagency Council on Crime Prevention and Improving Reentry. “While investigating crimes and prosecuting perpetrators must remain the top priority of law enforcement,” the Order states, “crime reduction policy should also include efforts to prevent crime in the first place and to lower recidivism rates.” Read more about the Executive Order and what the Interagency Council will do. At the center of America’s resurgence It’s impossible to talk about the record-breaking successes of the U.S. economy today without talking about tax cuts. Unemployment is at its lowest rate in 17 years. Jobless claims recently hit a 48-year low. In January alone, the U.S. economy added 200,000 jobs. “At the center of America’s resurgence are the massive tax cuts that we just passed before Christmas,” President Trump says. The evidence backs him up. Third-party consumer and small-business confidence indices have hit their highest marks in years this quarter. More than 4 million American workers are receiving bonuses, wage increases, or other new benefits as a direct result of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act. And the best part? As the positive impacts of tax reform multiply, more and more businesses have begun to turn their attention to the long-term investments that matter most for a healthy economy. Learn more about how tax reform is delivering for all Americans. Photo of the Day Vice President Mike Pence | March 7, 2018 (Official White House Photo by D. Myles Cullen) POTUS TODAY This morning, President Trump will hold a meeting with his Cabinet. Later, the President, along with Vice President Pence, will host a roundtable with members of Congress and video game industry leaders.

name: Anne Holton
subject: Pressing for Progress on Women's Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Derek -

[Today is International Women's Day, a chance to commemorate the social, political, cultural, and economic achievements of women worldwide.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=7pawLdg%2Fw4XxS%2ByPsLCY52oGR4Xdb%2FHg) But even as we celebrate all the progress women have achieved, we can't ignore how much further we have to go.

The theme for International Women's Day is "Press for Progress," and I can't imagine a better message to reflect the work women have done this past year - and the work we will continue to do in the years ahead.

From the #MeToo and #TimesUp movements taking on sexual assault and harassment, to the Women's March and grassroots organizations helping women across the country get more involved in politics and harness their political power, we're seeing so many women stand up and speak out against the broken systems that have allowed inequalities to go unchallenged for too long. **These women are pressing for progress - and I know my husband is standing with them.**

**In an essay he wrote for Esquire in the fall about why men must stand up for women 's equality, Tim illustrated one of the many reasons I'm so proud to have him as my husband and partner. [I hope you'll look up the article (if you haven't already!) and sign on to stand with us as we fight for progress >>](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=mRpOQlVOFOZ11MJvfjoGdmoGR4Xdb%2FHg)** | ["We need to take action. The truth is, right now in the year 2017, women--and women of color and LGBTQ+ women in even greater numbers--face a daily reality of sexism, harassment, and assault. And every man must examine how he can improve that reality. I'm ready to use the platform I have to speak up for those whom some would rather silence and stand up for those whom some would rather strike down."

_Esquire_ | "Why Men Need to Step Up for Women's Equality - and How I Plan to do My Part" by Tim Kaine | 11/02/2017 ](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=KZosY1aKSCA%2FY8KU5zk7R8y%2F7zedm4BQ)
---
I know Tim will always press for progress - it's what he's spent his life doing. When we had young kids, Tim supported me when I pursued a career as a judge. I've seen Tim hire, promote, and mentor countless women staffers and appoint strong women to public office throughout his entire career. At a time when women's rights are facing attacks from people in America's highest offices, we need every single ally to continue standing up, fighting back, and giving women a platform to advocate from. And I know Tim's with us.

[If you agree with Tim and me that we must never stop fighting for true equality, add your name and stand with us as we continue to press for progress. ](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=%2Bmjasg%2BVI5pC1GZnATv6eWoGR4Xdb%2FHg)

Thank you.

_Anne_

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: difficult times
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | It warmed my heart and lifted my spirits to see thousands of Americans take to the streets this year in the second-annual Women's March. We can't afford to take a single shred of our hard-fought progress for granted. Today, we have another opportunity to not only celebrate the progress we've made but advance it. It's International Women's Day -- and it couldn't come at a more important time. **Unless Congress takes action, the Violence Against Women Act will run out of funding in September.** This landmark piece of legislation is a life-saver. Since its original passage, domestic violence cases are down by more than 65% nationally. But we live in difficult times. Republicans in Congress and the Trump administration are working hand-in-hand to deal a blow to women's rights. They've targeted access to birth control and coverage of maternity care. **Now, Mike Pence is pledging that a woman 's right to choose will end in our lifetime. We cannot relent in our struggle to achieve equality.** [_**Make your voice heard, my friend. Call on Congress to reauthorize the Violence Against Women Act and commit yourself to defending women 's rights in the age of Trump.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f97/622b7b5b/19b940a4/307bb0f6/624984652/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180307b") | | | [Sign your Name](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f97/622b7b5b/19b940a4/307bb0f6/624984652/VEsE/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180307b")
---
Thank you,
Debbie

name: Megan West
subject: It's Women's History Month
body:
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180308qna01&n=1)
Hi Peter,
Today is the start of “Women's History Month.” This annual month-long event celebrates the contributions of women to society throughout history.
Since we have now entered into another election year, we thought we would look at the history of women in politics.
Do you know who was the first woman to run for President of the United States as a party's nominee? Make your selection below and see if you are right:
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Ffirst-woman-to-run-for-president-of-the-united-states%2F%3Fa%3D1&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180308qna01&n=2)
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Ffirst-woman-to-run-for-president-of-the-united-states%2F%3Fa%3D2&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180308qna01&n=3)
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Ffirst-woman-to-run-for-president-of-the-united-states%2F%3Fa%3D3&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180308qna01&n=4)
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Ffirst-woman-to-run-for-president-of-the-united-states%2F%3Fa%3D4&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180308qna01&n=5)
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Ffirst-woman-to-run-for-president-of-the-united-states%2F%3Fa%3D5&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180308qna01&n=6)
Here's another bit of history in the making. In 2018, America will vote to elect 33 Senators and all 435 members of the House of Representatives. But in addition, America will elect 209 state executives, which includes 36 governors, 30 lieutenant governors, 26 secretaries of state, and more. America will also elect 35 state attorney generals, 6,066 state legislators, 74 state supreme court judges, 224 state appellate court judges, and thousands of municipal council members and school board members (Source: [Ballotpedia.org](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.ballotpedia.org&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180308qna01&n=7)).
And let's not forget about the local school board elections. Over 1/3 of students in America will be affected by the school board elections in 2018.
It's your vote and your voice, so don't be silent in this critical midterm election.
**United. We Stand.**
Megan West

| | Peter -- Today is International Women's Day: our chance, every year, to reflect on women's extraordinary accomplishments, in Ohio and across the globe. Normally, this would call for a celebration of all we've accomplished in the push for equality, but the fact that this day falls this week -- 24 hours before Vice President Mike Pence is scheduled to appear in Cleveland with Congressman Jim Renacci -- is bringing down my mood. To say that Pence doesn't exactly have a great track record when it comes to fighting for women would be an understatement. He's consistently pushed for legislation restricting women's access to reproductive health services. He's said women shouldn't serve in the military. clean_body: | | | Peter -- Today is International Women's Day: our chance, every year, to reflect on women's extraordinary accomplishments, in Ohio and across the globe. Normally, this would call for a celebration of all we've accomplished in the push for equality, but the fact that this day falls this week -- 24 hours before Vice President Mike Pence is scheduled to appear in Cleveland with Congressman Jim Renacci -- is bringing down my mood. To say that Pence doesn't exactly have a great track record when it comes to fighting for women would be an understatement. He's consistently pushed for legislation restricting women's access to reproductive health services. He's said women shouldn't serve in the military. He sponsored the first bill to defund Planned Parenthood. And now he's throwing his support behind Sherrod's opponent, Congressman Jim Renacci, who's been a strong ally for this White House. name: Andrew Gillum
subject: One was too many
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3163/9e00690/45235533/2403a88b/3656330229/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/")
Hello,

Newtown, Pulse, Las Vegas, Parkland -- each one could have been the last mass shooting in our country's modern history. And every day our cities suffer acts of gun violence that never make cable news. For far too long, politicians have done far too little to address both these mass shootings and the daily losses of life in some communities.

I don't want my children to grow up in a world where gun violence is the norm, so our country must do more to prevent this epidemic from continuing. But, thanks to a 22-year-old-law, the federal government's hands are tied on what kind of research they can do.

The 1996 Dickey Amendment banned the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) from conducting public health research on gun violence. But I won't sit by and let this epidemic continue killing our children -- which is why I'm joining other elected officials and gun reform advocates around the country to bring more attention to this outdated law.

**[Can I count on you to add your name today to stand with us in demanding the repeal of the Dickey Amendment?](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3163/9e00690/45235533/2403a88a/3656330229/VEsE/ "http://sign.actiontaker.org/page/s/repeal-dickey?source=gillum&redirect_url=http://sign.actiontaker.org/314-repeal-dickey-gillum-ab")**

Repealing the Dickey Amendment is a no-brainer as the first step towards passing common sense gun reform. The CDC collects data on many kinds of violence, including child abuse, youth violence, suicides and sexual assaults, so why shouldn't it collect data on gun violence?

We cannot let this continue to be the norm just because the NRA -- and the lawmakers who receive money from them -- are scared of what the CDC's results may be.

**[Research on gun violence is long overdue. Add your name to demand Congress repeal the Dickey Amendment.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3163/9e00690/45235533/2403a88a/3656330229/VEsF/ "http://sign.actiontaker.org/page/s/repeal-dickey?source=gillum&redirect_url=http://sign.actiontaker.org/314-repeal-dickey-gillum-ab")**

Thank you,

Andrew

name: Team Donnelly
subject: Joe's still fighting
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
The cable news shows might've moved on from Mitch McConnell's attempts to gut our health care, but his Washington cronies sure haven't. |
---

| | Peter,

The cable news shows might've moved on from Mitch McConnell's attempts to gut our health care, but his Washington cronies sure haven't.

Take a look at their latest attempts to undermine our nation's health care system:
* **First** , in December, McConnell and Senate Republicans forced through a tax plan that will **increase insurance premiums** and will lead to as many as 13 million fewer Americans having health care.
* **Then** , in January, the administration **gave the green light for insurers to issue skimpier health care plans that can _leave out coverage_** for things like mental health care and pediatric services.
* **Now** , the administration released a proposal for short-term health care plans that would **allow insurers to discriminate against people based on pre-existing conditions, raise costs for seniors, charge consumers more when they 're sick, and let insurance providers opt out of covering essential benefits** including emergency services and preventative care.

These repeated attempts by McConnell to repeal and undermine the Affordable Care Act do nothing but hurt Americans. Joe won't give up the fight to protect our health care, and we're relying on you to stand with him.

**[Will you add your name to stand with Joe in support of the protecting affordable health care?](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c233c/7377f838/309186c2/51faff98/4168303997/VEsH/ "https://donnelly.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/support-the-aca")**

We can't afford to forget about this fight and move on. That's exactly what McConnell and his special interest allies want. They're waiting for folks like us to stop paying attention so they can continue to deliberately dismantle our health care system one piece at a time.

**We can 't let our guard down for one second, Peter.**

**[Add your name: Tell lawmakers to protect the Affordable Care Act>>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c233c/7377f838/309186c2/51faff98/4168303997/VEsE/ "https://donnelly.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/support-the-aca")**

Thanks,

Hey --

Did you have a chance to sign your name in support of Planned Parenthood yet?

3,137 of your neighbors did -- including Governor Wolf and I! Now we're counting on you to join us.

[**Add your name to the list of Pennsylvanians who support Planned Parenthood and a woman's right to choose.**](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecffe/73a2bc12/3e0a6353/1fccc2c3/3270846875/VEsH/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/support-planned-parenthood?utm_campaign=em18_180308_ppfa2_adv_gq_ndn__td&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180308_ppfa2_adv_gq_ndn__td&utm_content=1+-+Add+your+name+to+the+list+of+Pennsylvani")

As a volunteer and clinic escort, Tom helped to make sure women didn't have to live in fear of extremists who wanted to deny them their rights. Now, as governor, he's making sure Harrisburg Republicans don't enact the most extreme anti-choice laws in the country-- and that they don't defund important organizations like Planned Parenthood.

name: Alyssa Milano
subject: Without women
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
#
Friends,
Progress is impossible without change -- and that change is being led by women all over the country and in Maryland.
When you look to the woman to your left and your right, I want you to realize that **that, right there, is what democracy looks like**. It doesn't happen automatically. It demands our action and participation.
Like many of you, I decided to take action because I had enough of watching our politics be determined by the lowest common denominator. It was outrageous to me that someone who treats women as objects could become the President of the United States. But I know that it is not just about who's sitting in the Oval Office.
But, as we've seen in Virginia and Alabama, we can fight back when we vote for and rely on our state and local representatives to look out for the best interests of underrepresented and marginalized communities.**That is why I support Ben, because I know he will prioritize helping those that need it the most in Maryland.**
**[In honor of Women's History Month, and International Women's Day, stand with me and join Women for Ben: a coalition of women dedicated to bringing change, strength, and diversity seeing this man bring strength and diversity back to Maryland.](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a75b/7ab0e2c4/7f5ea367/349a0637/1938453505/VEsH/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/women-for-ben?source=OHQ03088C")**
You and I both know that no one will win in 2018 without the support of women who are marching, organizing, and voting.
After the election, so many of us found a voice and a community in the resistance. In 2017, women stood together and said #MeToo and #TimesUp. We gathered in record numbers to march in solidarity for the protection of our rights, safety, health, and our families. We overcame our collective fears to break the silence. **And we voted.**
**[We need to keep up this momentum in 2018. That's why I need you to join me in becoming a Woman for Ben.](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a75b/7ab0e2c4/7f5ea367/349a0637/1938453505/VEsE/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/women-for-ben?source=OHQ03088C")**
2017 was not easy, but we saw that voting is how we prove our country is stronger and kinder than the man that is in the White House. Your vote is your voice, friends. In a democracy like ours, the real power is **with you**. That's why I am so hopeful for 2018: because we have the opportunity to show the real power of the people and elect men and women to our local governments who represent the real interests of our states.
We formed a movement that is unstoppable, and when the time comes time to vote, we're going to prove that it's also unbeatable.
Thank you,
******[CHIP IN](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a75b/7ab0e2c4/7f5ea367/349a0636/1938453505/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bj-st3-18?refcode=OHQ03088C")******

* * *
PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
---

Email is one of the most critical tools for our campaign, but you can let us know if you would like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a75b/7ab0e2c4/7f5ea367/349a0630/1938453505/VEsD/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=emfooter") going forward. name: =?utf-8?Q?Team=20Greitens?=
subject: "Unlike anything I’ve ever seen in all my years of law enforcement"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
body:
\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Team,
Please see the below piece that raises several good questions. **Please share with your friends on[Facebook](https://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=f4c152eadb&e=84041254e6) and [Twitter](https://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=b579139e1e&e=84041254e6).**

[](https://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=67a8092c5c&e=84041254e6)
By: Dave Grossman
March 8, 2018
Below is a portion of the article. [Read the full piece here.](https://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=f5d7c615d5&e=84041254e6)

Five questions about the legitimacy of this case demand an answer.
**1\. Where is the police report?**
Our legal system typically works like this: someone commits a crime against someone else. The victim calls the police, who file a report and conduct an investigation. A prosecutor uses the police documents to get an indictment, and a trial begins.

That's how the system is supposed to work. But in this case, the indictment came down without a police report. It came without a full investigation. And -- astonishingly -- it came without a victim seeking justice. We know, from prior reporting, that the person who the Circuit Attorney has alleged is the victim never went to the police and never brought an action against Greitens. Indeed, her only complaint has been that this case was brought into the public to begin with. She asked for privacy, and the prosecutor gave her a circus.

Remember how this case first came to light: An aggrieved ex-husband recorded the victim without her consent or knowledge. A television station, Missouri's KMOV, had shabby enough editorial standards that they chose to run that recording on the nightly news, even as other news organizations passed on it. And that, it turns out, is all that was used by the prosecutor to bring a charge: a secret recording by an angry ex-husband, and a news report.

That's hardly the stuff of which felony indictments should be made.

**2\. Where is the evidence?**
Gardner's allegation depends on one question: did Greitens take a compromising photo of the alleged victim in the spring of 2015 and transmit it? Through feints and hints in the media, the prosecution claimed to have such a photo. Some in the media believed them. Not so. Based on discovery delivered to the defense just a few days ago, the prosecution was forced to admit that it has no photo. The damning piece of evidence -- the keystone for their entire case -- doesn't exist.

So what other evidence do they have? None. Yes, you read that correctly: There is no other evidence in their possession. The prosecution admitted as much in testimony before the judge this past week. They pleaded with the judge to allow their office to let them have the summer to "gather the evidence."

Sorry, but Gardner has it backwards. Evidence is supposed to lead to an indictment, not the other way around.

**3\. Why did the FBI, the U.S. Attorney, and the police refuse to look into this case?**

In words she must surely want to take back, the spokesman for Gardner's office admitted to the St. Louis Post Dispatch, when asked about other law enforcement agencies who looked at this case, **" The police, the FBI and the U.S. attorney's office declined to investigate for various reasons."** That's telling: The indictment and takedown of a governor is a big deal for any law enforcement agency. And yet no one bit. Gardner shopped this case to other agencies, and those agencies saw no evidence of a crime and decided not to investigate.

That's also important because those institutions, particularly the U.S. Attorney and the FBI, are responsible for dealing with public corruption. In fact, they have a whole unit devoted to just that. Had there been evidence to suggest a crime by a high state official, surely they would have brought action. That none of them have is an important signal.

[**See the full list of questions here.**](https://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=6b90f7504a&e=84041254e6)

_Lt. Col. Dave Grossman (US Army, ret.) is author of the books, "On Killing," "On Combat" and "Assassination Generation." He has been featured on 60 Minutes and 20/20, and has been interviewed on many major networks._

\----
See the full piece here: [http://thefederalist.com/2018/03/08/five-questions-on-the-eric-greitens-indictment-that-must-be-answered/](https://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=d21f8e31d0&e=84041254e6)
[Share on Facebook](https://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=ca2d615d89&e=84041254e6)
[Share on Twitter](https://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=a0fc20b539&e=84041254e6)

Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer

Emails are one of the best ways to stay connected. If they become too much, you can [unsubscribe](https://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=3f71c40094&e=84041254e6&c=64af77983c).

| Friends -

A new poll just came out today, and I wanted to make sure you saw that Democrat Joe Donnelly is _officially in trouble_. In case you missed it, Democrat Donnelly [is now only polling at 45%](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9538ebee3d&e=1acd9bb3d6), with more than half of Hoosiers saying they would vote to fire him.

**We're still several months out from Election Day, but this poll sends a powerful message:_What we're doing is working_. **

With the help of folks like you, we've been telling our fellow Hoosiers about the Democrat Donnelly who says one thing in Indiana, and then votes differently in Washington. He's a Democrat who:
* Says that he wants to fix Obamacare, but then votes to keep it
* Says that "Trump voters are Donnelly voters," but then says that Trump voters aren't "who America is"
* Votes as a rubber stamp for the Schumer-Pelosi agenda on the issues that matter most
* Has never passed a bill during his 12 years in Congress
* Is ranked as the least effective Democrat in the U.S. Senate (and that's saying something!)

**At** _your_**Indiana Republican Party, we**[went on the airwaves statewide](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=2d3c5d9829&e=1acd9bb3d6)**in February -- far earlier than ever before -- with a radio ad asking why Democrat Donnelly lied to Hoosiers about the benefits of President Trump's tax cut. Democrat Donnelly voted against the raise you're seeing in your paycheck, claiming that tax reform wouldn't benefit average Hoosiers. It wasn't true then, and it's ****definitely**** not true now.**

We're in this fight together, and we need the full team -- especially Hoosiers like you -- working hard through November, talking to your friends and family, making phone calls and going door-to-door telling Hoosiers about the real Joe Donnelly ([sign up to volunteer HERE](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=deda7ac4be&e=1acd9bb3d6)).

When Election Day rolls around, the hard work of Hoosiers across the state will make sure the results match this poll we're seeing today!

-Kyle Hupfer
Chairman, Indiana Republican Party
